_note_1: PLACEHOLDER — not a functional OpenClaw plugin manifest. openclaw-turbocharger is a standalone OpenAI-compatible HTTP sidecar; the runtime adapter required for `openclaw plugins install` is not part of v0.1.0-alpha and is being designed in a separate package (see ADR-0027 in docs/DECISIONS.md and tracking issue #22).
_note_2: OpenClaw plugin model: a provider plugin is a TypeScript package that calls api.registerProvider(...) inside the OpenClaw process. Reference: https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/architecture#public-capability-model. Per ADR-0027, the turbocharger adapter will live as a separate npm package; whether it ships from this repo (subdirectory or pnpm workspace) or its own repo is intentionally undecided.
_note_3: OpenClaw users today: configure turbocharger as a custom OpenAI-compatible provider pointing at the sidecar's HTTP port. See README.md `Configuration` section.
